info.title: Keasy API
info.description: Keasy — data pipeline execution and cloud storage API

200.description: SSE stream: delta events + complete with GenerateResponse
SSE stream: delta events + complete with SuggestResponse

get.description:
Returns the authenticated user's profile, org, and effective role.
Protected by session_required but NOT tenant_context_required.

The workspaces the authenticated user belongs to, for the sidebar switcher.
Sourced from the `workspaces` token claim (captured at login) — no runtime
Keycloak call; membership is declared in Terraform.

get.summary:
List the workspace catalog: every registered dataset (a completed job's
output) with its types, columns and row counts. Governance metadata — open to
every member (the IDS/Solid model: members discover the space at the metadata
level, the bytes stay producer-scoped).

400.description: Invalid connection or container not found

200.description: List of files in the connection

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Relative file path within the connection

400.description: Schema inference failed or unsupported file type

patch.summary:
Browser-driven completion: the client (`@fossil-lang/executor`) ran the
mapping, signed-PUT the output, and reports the outcome here. `Completed`
stores the executor's `RunStatus` (the discovery + DCAT views read it); the
server never touches the data — only the metadata.

200.description: Job status updated from the browser run
GraphAr manifest YAMLs for the catalog dataset

404.description: Job not found or no catalog

200.description: GraphAr manifest YAMLs, keyed by dataset-relative path

404.description: Job not found or no output

200.description: Signed URLs for direct Parquet access

404.description: Job not found or no output

post.summary:
Sign PUT URLs so the browser uploads the GraphAr output it just produced
directly to the member's chosen destination (no data through the server).
The output lives at `{dest_base}/{job_id}/<key>` where `dest_base` is the
connection the member picked (`sink_connection_id`), or the substrate
fallback — the same dest the completion `RunStatus` reports.

200.description: Signed PUT URLs for the output keys

get.summary:
The job's connection ref-map (name → base URL). The browser feeds it to the
executor's `sources()`/`run()` to resolve `@conn` aliases. No credentials —
only the base URLs (signing is a separate, per-URL call).

200.description: Connection ref-map for the job's sources

post.summary:
Sign GET URLs so the browser fetches the program's cloud sources directly
(no data through the server). Each cloud URI is signed with the creds of the
job connection whose base URL prefixes it; non-cloud (HTTP/public) URIs pass
through verbatim.

200.description: Fetch URLs (signed GET for cloud) per source URI

explain.description:
When true, the LLM acts as a data analyst explaining query results
instead of generating SQL. The question should contain the original
question, SQL, and result rows.

schema.description:
DuckDB table schema from the frontend (column names, types, sample values).
When provided, this is used as context for SQL generation instead of the
pipeline summary.

sql.description: DuckDB SQL query generated by the LLM (executed client-side via DuckDB-WASM).

AskResultCode.description: Result code for AI ask responses — replaces raw string literals.

CatalogDataset.description: One registered dataset (a completed job's output) as the catalog sees it.

job_id.description: The job id (the `job_` schema suffix), the dataset's stable handle.

tables.description: One entry per registered vertex/edge type.

CatalogTable.description: A registered type within a dataset and its SQL shape.

rows.description: Row count from the Parquet footers (cheap — no full scan).

rdf_uri.description:
The full RDF predicate IRI this column maps (e.g.
`https://example.org/name`); `None` when the output has no RDF predicate.

xsd_datatype.description:
The XSD datatype IRI of the literal value (e.g.
`http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string`); `None` when not an RDF
literal. Part of the output spec the governance layer (DCAT) consumes.

CompleteJobRequest.description:
The browser-driven completion payload (PATCH `/v1/jobs/{id}`): after running
the mapping in the browser (`@fossil-lang/executor`) and uploading the output
by signed PUT, the client reports the run's outcome. `manifest` is the
executor's `RunStatus` — the SAME type the old subprocess produced — so the
discovery + DCAT paths consume it unchanged.

oneOf.description: The DCAT-AP catalog `RunStatus`, when the client also built the catalog.
The GraphAr `RunStatus` for the uploaded output (on `Completed`).

status.description: The terminal (or `Running`) status the client is transitioning the job to.

direction.description: Read source vs the workspace write sink. Defaults to `source`.
`source` (default) or `sink` (the owner output store; one per workspace).

sink_connection_id.description: The connection the member picked as the output destination (job config).

DataResponse_Value.description: Typed envelope for successful API responses: `{ "data": T }`.

datasets.description: Every registered dataset in the workspace catalog.

Direction.description:
Whether a connection is a READ source (programs reference it via `@conn`) or
the workspace's WRITE sink (where the owner's job output is materialised).
Orthogonal to [`ConnectionKind`] (which describes a source's data) and
[`LocationType`]: a connection is a named, credentialed storage location, and
`direction` says how it is used. Exactly one `sink` exists per workspace (the
owner output store); `kind` is source-only and ignored for a sink.

EdgeStatus.description: One edge type — its CSR/CSC Parquet pair and endpoints.

count.description: Edge count (Parquet footer metadata); `None` if the count query failed.

edge_type.description: The edge type / predicate local name.

oneOf.description:
DCAT-AP catalog graph structure (`fossil catalog` subprocess output):
per-type Parquet + counts, stored in owner cloud. Its `dest` is the
catalog base URL. Drives the catalog graph view (DuckDB-WASM reads the
Parquet directly).

created_by.description:
Keycloak `sub` of the member who created the job — the data-product owner.
Server-derived (never from the client); used for producer-scoped data
access (only the producer reads/runs the job's data) + DCAT publisher.

oneOf.description:
GraphAr structure from the fossil subprocess: per-type Parquet + row
count + columns, per-edge CSR/CSC pair. Its `dest` IS the dataset base
URL — fossil's single description of the output, so keasy keeps no
duplicate `rdf_base`. Column statistics are NOT here — the browser
computes them from the Parquet (DuckDB-WASM).

sink_connection_id.description:
Connection the member chose as the output destination (where the GraphAr
output lands). The producer owns where their data product goes — output is
signed with this connection's cloud creds, under `{conn.url}/{job_id}`.
`None` falls back to the workspace substrate (transitional).

JobRuntimeError.description:
Runtime job error — stored in the database as JSON on a failed job.
This is NOT an API error type; it is a serializable record of what went wrong during execution.

manifest_files.description:
The GraphAr manifest YAMLs, keyed by dataset-relative path
(`graph.graph.yml`, `vertex/Person.vertex.yml`, …). Fed verbatim into
`@fossil-lang/graph`'s `createGraphClient({ manifestFiles })`; keasy
treats them as opaque blobs — fossil owns the GraphAr layout.

paths.description:
Dataset-relative output keys the browser executor produced
(`vertex/Person.parquet`, `edge/<dir>/by_source.parquet`,
`graph.graph.yml`, …).

RunStatus.description: The status object `fossil run --output-json` writes to stdout.

dest.description: Destination URL the `GraphAr` dataset was written under (echoes `--dest`).

edges.description: One entry per emitted edge type (empty until a shape/Phase-B adds edges).

version.description: Wire-contract version this payload was produced with (see [`WIRE_VERSION`]).

vertices.description: One entry per emitted vertex type.

refs.description:
Connection ref-map `{ name: baseUrl }` — the browser passes it to the
executor (`@fossil-lang/executor`) so `@name/path` source aliases resolve
to `{baseUrl}/path`, identically to the native engine.

uris.description:
The RESOLVED source URIs the executor's `sources()` returned (already
`@conn`-resolved, e.g. `s3://bucket/prefix/users.csv`).

urls.description:
Each input URI → a fetch URL: a signed GET for cloud sources, or the URI
verbatim for public/HTTP ones. The browser fetches each and stages the
bytes for the executor under the SAME URI.

VersionResponse.description:
The running build's version — announced so operators (and the fleet view) can
see exactly which image a tenant is on. `git_sha`/`built_at` are stamped at
build time (CI sets `KEASY_GIT_SHA`/`KEASY_BUILT_AT`); `version` is the crate
version. See [[project_keasy_swarm_deploy_architecture]] (W0.6).

VertexStatus.description:
One vertex type — its dataset-relative Parquet, row count, and property
columns.

count.description: Row count (Parquet footer metadata); `None` if the count query failed.

rdf_type.description:
The full RDF type IRI the mapping declares for this vertex (the shape IRI,
e.g. `https://example.org/Person`); `None` when the output has no RDF
type. This is the output *spec* fossil owns — the governance layer (DCAT)
reads it from the manifest instead of re-deriving it from the program.

current.description: This instance's slug — the "current" entry in the switcher.

workspaces.description:
Slugs of every workspace the user belongs to, from the `workspaces` token
claim (captured at login). The web builds each `<slug>.<domain>` link.
